---
title: "Optional GitHub account setup"
subtitle: "Create a GitHub account and apply for free GitHub Copilot Pro access"
date: 2026-07-01
author:
  - name: Jelmer Poelstra
    orcid: 0000-0002-3514-7462
    email: poelstra.1@osu.edu
    affiliation: 
      - name: CFAES Bioinformatics Core, Ohio State University
        url: https://go.osu.edu/bioinformatics-core
number-sections: true
bibliography: refs.bib
---

---------

<hr style="height:1pt; visibility:hidden;" />

## Overview

For the purposes of this workshop,
a GitHub account will allow you to use the agentic AI tool GitHub Copilot within VS Code.

The following steps are not strictly required before the workshop,
because creating a GitHub account can be done quite seamlessly during the workshop,
and because you'll also have access to an alternative, similar AI tool (Claude Code).

However, we do recommend following the steps below, considering that:

- Claude Code is a paid tool that we will provide institutional^[for-fee, but free to you]
  access to during the workshop,
  but that you will not have access to after the workshop unless you pay for it in some way.

- While GitHub Copilot, on the other hand, cannot be used institutionally (i.e., with private data) at OSU,
  it does have a free 'personal' option for academic users.

As such, for many of you, GitHub Copilot may be the easiest way to continue trying out
agentic AI tools after the workshop (we'll discuss your options further during the workshop).

## Create a GitHub account (if you don't have one yet)

GitHub is a place where you can store your code and share it with others,
and where you can find code and software written by others.
It can therefore be very useful to have a GitHub account beyond GitHub Copilot usage.

1. Go to <https://github.com>

2. Click the "**_Sign Up_**" button all the way in the top right corner of the page

3. Fill out the form to provide your personal details
   (only use "Continue with Google" if you're happy linking this to your Google
   account rather than your OSU email address -- see below):
  
   - When choosing your **_username_**, best to keep it professional and include
     (parts of) your real name:
     you may use GitHub to share your code, like when publishing a paper
  
   - It's recommended to sign up with your **_OSU email address_**,
     as that will make the educational access verification process smoother

5. Check your email and click the link to **_verify your email address_**.
   There may also be some two-factor authentication steps to complete.

## Apply for free GitHub Copilot Pro access

The following will work for teachers/faculty and students alike –
you'll just need to select the appropriate option in the form:

1. Go to <https://github.com/settings/education/benefits>

2. Click "**Start an application**":

![](img/github-edu-benefits.png){fig-align="center" width="90%"}

3. Fill out the form as needed. Note that you will most likely need a document
   that proves your current status at the university.

::: {.callout-caution appearance="simple"}
Application approval is often quite fast (sometimes in minutes),
but **access is only granted 72 hours after approval**.
:::

## Plans

https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/concepts/billing/individual-plans

- Free: 2,000 code completions per month, ~50 chat requests 
- Student: unlimited code completions, 200 credits
- Pro: unlimited code completions, 1,500 credits
